msg: @uri113 what I understand (and yes I did face same situation, and raised it with team, like “additional feature” and didn’t get any response), when we generate a lease, and its not bound to a device , it neither stays in “leased” filter nor in “not leased” filter, so u really need to go in default, and go up and down all the way to search for it (because making search easier is another task at hand for team, in may be list of 2027). Now think it from POV, u made 20 such leases, out of lets say 3Nodes, and now u need to scroll through 600 licences and make them visible one by one. And every time u make one setting change in such leases, the licence tab resets and u need to re scroll and research the whole thing. Because the leases are not visible in leased or not leased until they are taken on a device and they keep their setting intact, even after revoking / or not revoking and not used…..and this was exact simple issue raised by me and “additional feature” requested. hope this communication is clear to team @ValaykaWBC

msg:
[Photo] 🤝 **Unity Network - Application & Web Updates**

The Unity Network web panel has received a major upgrade, with new features rolling out across web, Android, and iOS. This update focuses on bringing the web panel closer to feature parity with the app, as well as improvements to license management, communication, and general housekeeping.


Features, improvements, and general updates:
- Messaging is now available directly within the web panel
- Mass messaging for reaching your network efficiently
- Settings menu for the web app
- Pagination on licensing for smoother browsing of larger portfolios
- License search on the marketplace
- License alias support
- Marketplace browsing and claim flow - users can browse and claim before signing in
- Manage your owned licenses and lease life cycle directly from the web panel
- Custom sound for push notifications
- Additional anti-bot protection
- General improvements to web panel performance and stability


Web Panel: https://unitynodes.io/
Google Play: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=io.unitynodes.unityapp
Direct APK: releases.unitynodes.io/android/
App Store: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/unity-network-app/id6755482738


No uninstall required - update directly over your current version. 

🔗 https://x.com/UnityNodesIO/status/2044832019264200826

you can call runner numbers as many time as you want
I don't quite have the right answer for you
we were in alpha, they were testing things

usually you would pick call runner plan and call th e number it displays, 
if it fails, you can retry a few time through an option at the end of the call

usually when it is a success, it will give you a new number part of that range of number for that route

if you had a different phone with  different sim, same plan, you could stumble on that same number
In alpha, sometimes, they were making the number fixed for a bit for a runner plan while they were looking at issue with IVR
you could call as many times, it would not connect, no reward, they were able to look at issue in the background
it will all make sense when Public/beta gets released next week
hopefully there will be less of that
